data.content:
**Yes, C# fully supports `stackalloc`'d `Span` of native-sized integers.** Both `Span<nint>` and `Span<nuint>` work:

```csharp
Span<nint>  nints  = stackalloc nint[4];   // ✅ works
Span<nuint> nuints = stackalloc nuint[4];  // ✅ works
```

The test confirmed it compiles and runs correctly on .NET 8. On this 64-bit system, `nint.MaxValue` was `9223372036854775807` (Int64.MaxValue) and `nuint.MaxValue` was `18446744073709551615` (UInt64.MaxValue), as expected.
